text.title: MVP Instrumentation Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every MVP Instrumentation Checklist application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Checklist MUST cover ≥1 event each in acquire / activate / retain / revenue; missing any layer is rejected.
rule.rationale: Three-layer funnels collapse to vanity metrics; four-layer covers the standard pirate funnel (AARRR minus referral).

rule.statement: Each event MUST carry an explicit event_name string used in the analytics tool; 'we have signups' without event_name is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without named event, the dashboard cannot be queried programmatically.

rule.statement: Checklist MUST cite the dashboard URL where these events display; private-to-author dashboards are rejected.
rule.rationale: Dashboard URL gives downstream agents a single source to verify state.

rule.statement: Checklist MUST be marked 'pass' before launch event commits; failing checklist blocks the launch.
rule.rationale: Post-launch instrumentation is hypothesis-by-folklore; pre-launch is data-by-default.

rule.statement: Each filled instance carries version + last_reviewed; un-versioned instances are rejected.
rule.rationale: Re-parseable across analytics-tool migrations (PostHog → Plausible etc).
